title: Mesocoptus beccarii
author: Riedel, Alexander
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Mesocoptus beccarii  (Pascoe)      Coptorrhynchus beccarii  Pascoe, 1885: 213. Type locality: Kendari.       Mesocoptus beccarii  (Pascoe): Marshall, 1956: 121–123.       Description . Lectotype (Fig. 22a). Length. Pronotum plus elytron 5.40 mm. Integument black, covered with green scales; subglabrous black granules lending mottled appearance. Head with vertex microreticulate, squamose, middle of forehead with some radial wrinkles. Rostrum 1.28 X as long as wide, sides subparallel, weakly widening apicad. Pronotum ca. as long as wide (1.03 X); punctate-granulate. Elytra 1.32 X as long as wide; intervals with rows of subglabrous granules each bearing a long, erect bristles its length subequal an interval´s width; densely mottled with patches of green round appressed scales. Abdomen. Ventrites 1–2 at middle concave, without scales, coarsely punctate-rugose;sublaterally squamose.Ventrites3–5 nude except with sparse suberect setae, microreticulate; ventrite 5 medially with impression, coarsely punctate-rugose. Genitalia. Penis (Fig. 22b) with sides of body subparallel, apex subangulate, medially tip slightly extended, curved ventrad. Ostium large, at center of body. Apodeme 2.3 X as long as body of penis. Intraspecific variation. Length. Pronotum plus elytron 5.40–5.80 mm.       Material examined . Type specimens. Male, lectotype by present designation: INDONESIA, SE Sulawesi: Kendari, III.[18]74, O. Beccari, “12664”; labels (Fig. 22c); condition: fair, formerly pinned through right elytron; genitalia in glycerol in microtube; DNA extraction ARC7305 (GenBank # OR557458) (SMTD). Paralectotypes: 2 exx, DNA extraction ARC7304 (not sequenced), same data as lectotype (SMTD). Other material examined: INDONESIA, SE Sulawesi: 1 ex, ARC6764 (GenBank # OR541118), SE-Sulawesi, Kendari, Moramo, S04° 13.377´E122° 44.610´, 153 m, 16-IV-2013 (SMNK).       Notes . Pascoe (1885) based the original description on an unknown number of specimens; he did not designate a holotype. Three syntypes fitting his description were found at SMTD. A lectotype is designated to ensure stability of nomenclature.
